name: Sapiom
oneliner: Sapiom is building the financial infrastructure layer — identity, authentication, and micro-payment rails — that lets AI agents autonomously purchase software, APIs, and compute resources without human intervention.
finserv: Backed by Accel (lead), Okta Ventures, Gradient Ventures (Google), Anthropic, and Coinbase Ventures; founder Ilan Zerbib was Shopify's director of engineering for payments; enterprise customers already embedding Sapiom in production agent deployments
team: Founded by Ilan Zerbib (ex-Shopify Director of Engineering for Payments). Backed by Accel (lead), Okta Ventures, Gradient Ventures, Array Ventures, Menlo Ventures, Anthropic, and Coinbase Ventures. Total raised: $15.75M seed.
thesis: Sapiom directly maps onto the Agent Authentication & Security gap in your framework. The core thesis — that you want to be the regulated infrastructure layer agents connect to — requires that agents can be identified, authenticated, and given financial access in a compliant way. Sapiom is building exactly that stack for the commercial internet; the open question is whether they will build a regulated, compliance-grade version for financial services or whether that vertical remains open for your positioning.
